text:
# PROCESS — what I did this iteration (explainability; REQUIRED)

## Ranked issue list (clusters by # failing tasks × trials, biggest first)
| rank | cluster | tasks | shared root cause | tag | planned change class |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Cheapest flight not minimized — anchors on existing flights | 15 (0.50), 16 (0.60) | Agent reuses reservation's flight numbers instead of searching all options (direct + one-stop) and picking global minimum | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule (cheapest = global minimum procedure) |
| 2 | Multiple certificates used in booking | 14 (0.00) | Agent applies all 3 certificates despite max-1 policy; book_reservation has no guard | RULE VIOLATION | In-body guard (code) — reject >1 certificate |
| 3 | Basic economy flight change via update | 14 (0.00) | Agent uses update_reservation_flights on basic_economy reservation to change flight segments | RULE VIOLATION | In-body guard (code) — reject segment changes when cabin stays basic_economy |
| 4 | Communication gap — total not stated | 7 (0.00) | Agent completes multi-step writes but never states the total dollar amount ($1628 refund) | KNOWLEDGE/OUTPUT CONTRACT | Policy rule (multi-operation total communication) |
| 5 | Budget exceeded — unmentioned segments modified | 10 (0.40) | Agent rewrites return legs user didn't ask to change as workaround to meet budget | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule (scope of changes) |
| 6 | Per-passenger cabin upgrade attempted | 12 (0.80) | Agent fabricates workaround when told to upgrade one passenger (impossible — cabin is per-reservation) | KNOWLEDGE | Policy rule (cabin is per-reservation, all-or-nothing) |
| 7 | Wrong baggage total_baggages value | 15 (0.50) | Agent passes total_baggages=1 when user said "total of four" | BEHAVIORAL/KNOWLEDGE | Policy rule (baggage arithmetic) + in-body guard (code) |

## Changes made this iteration (one row per edit)
| cluster | edit class | file / tool | what & why it generalizes | protects passing? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2 (multi-cert) | in-body guard (code) | tools.py / book_reservation | Rejects booking if >1 travel certificate in payment_methods. General: any booking attempt with multiple certs is caught. | Yes — no passing task books with >1 certificate (policy max is 1, all gold actions comply). |
| 3 (basic econ flight change) | in-body guard (code) | tools.py / update_reservation_flights | Rejects when reservation.cabin=="basic_economy" AND cabin param=="basic_economy" AND flights differ from current. General: any basic economy segment change is blocked. Does NOT fire for cabin upgrades (cabin != basic_economy). | Yes — task 11 is economy (not basic_economy) → guard does NOT fire. No other passing task calls this tool on a basic_economy reservation while keeping basic_economy cabin. |
| 7 (baggage reduce) | in-body guard (code) | tools.py / update_reservation_baggages | Rejects if total_baggages < current reservation.total_baggages. General: "can only add, not remove" enforced at code level. | Yes — no passing task reduces bags (policy forbids removal). |
| 4 (communication) | output contract (policy) | policy.md / after-write reporting | Multi-operation total: state EACH charge/refund AND net total at end. General for all multi-write interactions. | Yes — passing tasks already report amounts; additive requirement to state sums. No behavior change, only output shape. |
| 1 (cheapest flight) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Change flights section | "Cheapest" means global minimum: MUST search both direct AND one-stop, compare all, pick strict minimum. Never anchor on existing flight numbers. | Yes — no passing task involves "cheapest" selection that was previously incorrect. Tasks that already pass use the correct flights. |
| 5 (scope lock) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Change flights section | Only modify segments user explicitly named. Never change unmentioned legs as workaround. | Yes — passing task 11 changes only the legs user specified. No passing task relies on modifying extra segments. |
| 6 (per-passenger cabin) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Change cabin section | Cabin is per-reservation: can't upgrade one passenger only. All upgrade together or none. | Yes — no passing task involves per-passenger cabin changes. |
| 7 (baggage arithmetic) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Change baggage section | total_baggages = new TOTAL (not delta). nonfree = max(0, total − free_allowance). | Yes — passing tasks already use correct baggage values. |
| 2 (cert limit policy) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Payment section | Emphasizes max 1 certificate even with multiple available; system rejects >1. | Yes — no passing task uses >1 certificate. Narrowing/reinforcing existing rule. |
| 3 (basic econ cancel+rebook) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Change flights section | Basic economy + flight changes = cancel + rebook path. Tool will REJECT segment changes. | Yes — passing tasks don't change basic economy flight segments. |

## Verify-the-fix (one line per change)
- Certificate guard: task 14 agent passes certificates _3765853 ($500), _9984806 ($250), _2765295 ($250) → cert_count=3 > 1 → ValueError fires with actionable message. Passing tasks that book: tasks 3, 4 use 1 certificate max → guard does NOT fire.
- Basic economy guard: task 14 reservation K1NW8N has cabin="basic_economy". If agent calls update with cabin="basic_economy" and different flights → guard fires. If agent calls with cabin="business" (upgrade), guard does NOT fire (cabin != basic_economy), allowing the upgrade path. Passing task 11 (GV1N64, cabin="economy") → guard does NOT fire.
- Baggage guard: task 15/t0 agent writes total_baggages=1 for reservation with current total_baggages ≥ 1 → if current > 1, guard fires. For correct writes (total=4 > current), guard does NOT fire. Passing tasks that update bags always add → guard does NOT fire.
- Communication rule: task 7 agent completes upgrade ($1072 charge) + cancel ($1628 refund). New rule requires stating each amount AND net total. Agent now prompted to say "$1628 refund." Passing tasks already state amounts — additive requirement doesn't change behavior.
- Cheapest flight rule: task 15/16 agent should search direct+onestop for ATL→PHL on 2024-05-24, find HAT110+HAT172 ($207 total) vs HAT227+HAT139 ($216). Must pick $207 minimum. Passing tasks don't involve "cheapest" selection errors.
- Scope-of-changes: task 10 agent rewrites return legs user didn't mention → new rule blocks this. Passing task 11: user asks to change specific legs → agent changes only those → rule is satisfied.
- Per-passenger cabin: task 12 agent fabricates separate booking for one passenger → new rule says cabin is all-or-nothing. Passing tasks don't attempt per-passenger upgrades.
- Baggage arithmetic: task 15 user says "total of four" → agent should write total_baggages=4, nonfree = max(0, 4-free). Rule clarifies the semantics. Passing tasks already use correct values.

## Process & features used
- Subagents: 4 parallel diagnostic subagents to analyze trajectory clusters (always-failing + flaky tasks)
- Prior iterations read: cand_0005 PROCESS.md + diff.patch (built on all its improvements), cand_0004 (rejected, avoided Write Discipline / calculate mandate), cand_0002 (rejected, avoided cancel eligibility guard)
- Built on cand_0005: kept ALL its improvements intact (route guard, landed/flying guard, user_id derivation, budget verification, conservative cancellation, prior-flight lookup, social pressure resistance)

## Good things to PRESERVE
- All cand_0005 improvements (route guard, landed-flight guard, user_id derivation, budget verification, conservative cancellation, social pressure, prior-flight lookup)
- All cand_0003 improvements (get_all_reservation_details loop tool, DOB case logic, date-only rule, communication of actual amounts, NYC mapping)
- All cand_0001 improvements (compute_free_checked_bags, get_flight_details, mandatory first steps)
- cancel_reservation docstring keeping "Before calling, verify..." (NOT "This tool ENFORCES")

## Deliberately skipped (cluster + why)
- Task 44 (0.00, multi-upgrade efficiency): Gold expects 3 upgrades but agent runs out of steps (MAX_STEPS). Root cause is step-budget exhaustion from excessive search calls, not a policy/tool gap. A composite "batch_upgrade" tool would help but adds complexity and risk. Deferred to next iteration.
- Task 9 (0.60, cancel ineligible reservation): Full eligibility guard in cancel_reservation was REFUTED in cand_0002 (broke 13/34/43 via docstring reasoning change). The landed-flight guard (cand_0005) already catches the most common case. Not worth regression risk for 0.40 gain.
- Task 5 (0.90): Near ceiling. Risk outweighs potential 0.10 gain.
- Task 8 (0.90): Near ceiling. Risk outweighs potential 0.10 gain.
